channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Feel the heat? Everything you need to know about Couleur Café 2026
item.description: Brussels' Couleur Café music festival will bring the heat in more ways than one when it returns under high temperatures this weekend.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 26 - 28 June
item.description: While a heatwave has not stopped a weekend full of festivals, performances and expositions. Here are our favourite picks!

item.title: Another record broken: Hottest 25 June since records began
item.description: The mercury climbed to 34.2 degrees on Thursday at the official measuring station in Uccle.

item.title: Brussels residents will have to wait for bin collections as rubbish piles up in the streets
item.description: "We completely understand that this is particularly unpleasant for residents," Bruxelles-Propreté spokesperson Adel Lassouli told The Brussels Times.

item.title: Parks, swimming pools and drinking fountains: here’s where to cool off in the capital
item.description: To help residents cope with the extreme heat, the City of Brussels has launched an interactive map highlighting the capital's main cooling spots.

item.title: Brussels gives out water to homeless people during heatwave
item.description: "We’re helping them find their way to public drinking fountains or to shade in Brussels’ green spaces."

item.title: Brussels landlords update rent scale: What does this mean?
item.description: Getting reliable information on affordable rent in Brussels may become more complicated after the landlord union updated its "rent scale" this week.

item.title: Brussels residents swim in park spring over lack of open-air bathing spots
item.description: Campaigners brought together dozens for a swim on Wednesday evening in the 'seals basin' of the former 19th-century zoo of Brussels.

item.title: Cinema under the stars: Where to watch free films outdoors in Brussels this summer
item.description: Brussels prides itself on an abundance of free outdoor film screenings over the summer months, making for the ultimate cinematic experience.

item.title: Five things you're probably doing wrong to stay cool in the heat
item.description: What Belgians can learn from countries where personal heat management has been a way of life for centuries.

item.title: 'Incomprehensible': Belgians missing out on EU energy support due to Brussels Government delays
item.description: Belgium still cannot access €1.66 billion in EU funding to ease people's energy bills – because the Brussels Government has not done its part yet.

item.title: Schoolboy arrested for selling drugs in Ixelles
item.description: Residents and shopkeepers in the neighbourhood reported various disturbances at the end of last week.

item.title: Fancy holidaying at De Wever's house? Fake Airbnb profile set up at Belgian PM's address
item.description: During the two months it was live, the listing described the building as an "ideal pied-à-terre for an unforgettable stay" in Brussels.

item.title: Woman injured in suspected stabbing in Uccle
item.description: The victim was taken to hospital, and no further details were released regarding her condition.

item.title: Brussels' Atomium closes due to extreme heat, as SNCB cancels 100 trains per day
item.description: From Wednesday, maximum temperatures in most places will reach or even exceed 35°C, peaking at 38°C on Friday.

item.title: The man who invented the word 'Brexit' on his bittersweet legacy
item.description: "I'm a quiz question these days. Who invented the word 'Brexit'? Is it Boris Johnson, Nigel Farage, or an obscure failed politician-solicitor called Peter Wilding?"

item.title: How much money will Belgian households spend on their holidays this year?
item.description: Nearly half of single Belgians are planning to holiday in Belgium this year, as the cost premium for travelling alone drives staycations.

item.title: Man stabbed several times in the face in Brussels
item.description: The incident occurred around 09:00 near the tunnel on Rue des Vétérinaires.

item.title: Brussels rubbish collection disrupted due to Tuesday's public sector strike
item.description: Developments related to tonight’s waste collections should be monitored closely on Bruxelles-Propreté.

item.title: Etterbeek expresses 'serious concerns' over federal plans for migrant home raids
item.description: The Brussels municipal council's stance carries particular significance as MR, CD&V, and Les Engagés are part of the federal majority backing the bill.
